content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (May 17 - 23, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The week of May 17–23 was defined by the successful release of v2.0.2 and a major architectural shift toward a robust OWNER+AGENT identity model. Development focused on stabilizing the elizaOS Live USB and cloud infrastructure, transitioning agent lifecycle operations to a reliable job-queue system, and hardening cross-platform integration for mobile and desktop environments. These efforts significantly improved system reliability, security, and the scalability of agent deployments across Web2 and Web3 ecosystems.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Identity & Connector Architecture
A primary focus this week was the implementation of an OWNER+AGENT registry schema, enabling connectors to manage independent user and agent identities.
- **Identity Integration:** Enabled end-to-end OWNER+AGENT roles for X (Twitter) via Eliza Cloud OAuth ([#7851](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7851)) and updated OAuth callbacks for Linear, Calendly, and Shopify ([#7820](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7820)).
- **UI/UX:** Introduced role-aware UI components to support these new identity flows ([#7819](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7819)).

### Cloud Infrastructure & Lifecycle
The team moved to decouple core services from worker processes to improve system stability.
- **Job Queue Migration:** Migrated agent lifecycle operations—including suspend, restart, and snapshotting—to a job queue ([#7810](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7810), [#7815](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7815)).
- **API Enhancements:** Finalized cloud deployment endpoints ([#7804](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7804)) and implemented non-fatal error handling for billing records to prevent service interruptions ([#7794](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7794)).

### OS, Runtime, and Hardware
Significant progress was made in hardening the elizaOS Live USB and optimizing local inference.
- **Live OS:** Advanced the Live USB architecture with signed manifests and systemd-based health checks ([#7762](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7762)).
- **Hardware Validation:** Confirmed M-RoPE compatibility for Qwen3.5 speculative decoding on Intel Lunar Lake platforms ([#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631)) and added a `--low-vram-smoke` preset for 2B SFT training on consumer GPUs ([#7805](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7805)).
- **Cross-Platform:** Improved Android native integration and resolved boot deadlocks in Linux local-source startup ([#7781](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7781), [#7772](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7772)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
- **Cloud API Stability:** Resolved [#7794](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7794), fixing 500 errors during non-streaming chat completions by making billing record insertion non-fatal and improving database logging.
- **Hardware/Inference:** Validated speculative decoding performance on Lunar Lake iGPUs, identifying previous bandwidth issues as thermal/workload artifacts rather than kernel bugs ([#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638)).
- **Community:** Closed the SunfishLoop integration discussion ([#7758](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7758)).

### New & Active Issues
- **Release Blockers:** [#7837](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7837) highlights startup failures in release workflows due to missing platform-signing secrets.
- **Benchmarking:** [#7836](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7836) identifies a need to enable live Hyperliquid harness runs, currently blocked by demo-mode output rejection.
- **OpenVINO:** Discussions remain active regarding the OpenVINO runtime ([#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633)), with consensus leaning toward scoping it specifically for ASR (Whisper) workloads rather than LLM generation.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The project demonstrated high velocity this week, particularly in the transition to the v2.0.2 release. The volume of PRs related to identity and cloud infrastructure suggests a coordinated effort to standardize the agent-owner relationship. The community is actively engaged in refining the CI/CD pipeline, as evidenced by the large batch of dependency updates and the ongoing work to support diverse hardware architectures like RISC-V and ARM64.
